pr.title: Improve matching across multiple source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Large cross-system matching cleanup that improved GALE01 matched code by about 0.98% (+38104 bytes) and matched data by about 0.15% (+1760 bytes), with 51 new matches, 233 unmatched-item improvements, and no broken matches or regressions reported. The changes touched 98 files across Dolphin THP, baselib, camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, item, menu, and library code. Review cleanup pushed the PR toward maintainable decomp style: grouped switch dispatch where appropriate, no new register/asm-style keyword hacks, no optimizer pragmas, and preservation of inline float/string literals instead of symbolizing constants solely for matching.
what_changed: Improved many near-matching units across 98 files, producing 51 new exact matches and 233 improvements in still-unmatched items, with the local report listing 0 broken matches and 0 regressions.

Added and used offset/view structs in THPDec.c, including THPMCURowFields, THPFileInfoMCUBufferView, and DCT component predDC views, to access fields at known offsets and improve matching of THP decompression and Huffman decode routines.

Adjusted THPDec.c type details such as changing a Huffman table result variable from int to u8 and storing num_Vij through a u16 cast into pad2, indicating matching depended on exact width and memory layout.
Reworked THP MCU row decompression code to use info-relative MCU buffer pointers and typed view casts for MCUsPerRow, RST/currMCU/nMCU, predDC fields, and decode output pointers.

Camera code received stack/local ordering and control-flow rewrites, including collapsing conditional checks with abs_threshold_inline and changing some transform accesses to explicit cam->transform or cam->transform_copy fields.
ftCo_09F7 review cleanup converted a goto-like dispatch structure into a grouped switch while preserving matching behavior.
Reviewer-requested cleanup removed register usage from ftCo_Bury.c and gm_19EF.c.
Reviewer-requested cleanup restored inline float and string literals in ftcoll.c, gm_1832.c, gm_1A4C.c, gmregclear.c, and ifprize.c rather than introducing separate named constants.
Reviewer-requested cleanup removed a newly added optimizer pragma in gm_1832.c and removed new M2C_FIELD usage in grinishie1.c.

Verification comments report ninja build/GALE01/src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.o, full ninja, main.dol OK, and decomp-orchestrator regression checks passing with 0 metric regressions, 0 broken matches, and 0 fuzzy regressions.
smart_moves: Used narrow, local view structs for THPFileInfo layout-sensitive fields instead of broadly redefining the full SDK struct, allowing offset-correct code generation while limiting header churn.
Converted review-identified goto dispatch in ftCo_09F7 into a grouped switch, improving readability without giving up the match.
Kept inline literals after review because link-time constant pooling/relocation can resolve matching needs without making source less readable.
Backed out fragile matching aids such as register keywords, optimizer pragmas, and M2C_FIELD when reviewers flagged them, preserving project style and future maintainability.
Validated both targeted object build and full DOL/regression metrics after a particle context-slot type fix, reducing risk in a large multi-file matching PR.
decomp_lessons: When a function appears to be a dispatch table or chain of labeled blocks, try grouped switch statements before settling for goto-heavy recovered control flow.
Do not introduce register, asm, or similar keyword hacks just to influence codegen unless the project explicitly accepts them; reviewers rejected new register usage here.
Avoid replacing readable inline floats or strings with named data symbols purely for matching; reviewers noted these constants are resolved during linking and should generally stay inline.
Avoid adding optimizer pragmas as matching shortcuts in ordinary source; a new global_optimizer-off pragma was explicitly rejected.
Offset/view structs can be useful for matching binary layouts when the current public struct is incomplete or not laid out as codegen expects, but they should be targeted and evidence-driven.
For wide cross-file matching sweeps, include regression-check evidence with broken matches, metric regressions, and fuzzy regressions, not just a list of local code edits.
Exact integer widths and store widths matter: THPDec matching improved with u8 result and u16 writes into packed/padded fields.
naming_conventions: Existing anonymous or address-based names such as fn_80165AC0, ftCo_8009F834, grKongo_801D7134, and gm_801BAD70 were preserved where stronger semantic names were not evidenced.

Temporary THP layout helper structs were named descriptively by subsystem and view purpose, e.g. THPMCURowFields, THPFileInfoMCUBufferView, THPFileInfoDCTCompYView, THPFileInfoDCTCompUView, and THPFileInfoDCTCompVView.
Reviewer feedback favored keeping literal strings inline in arrays, rather than creating many address-style names such as gm_803DB640 solely to reference string data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Stack and local declaration ordering was adjusted in files such as camera.c to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Control-flow reshaping was used for matching, including negated compound conditions and grouped switch dispatch instead of scattered gotos.
Typed pointer casts and narrow view structs were used to force field access at expected offsets in THPDec.c.
Pointer aliases such as an info-relative MCU buffer pointer were introduced to match generated addressing patterns.
Field access spelling was changed in camera code from transform pointer writes to explicit cam->transform/cam->transform_copy writes where that improved code generation.
Rejected tactics included register keyword insertion, optimizer pragmas, M2C_FIELD additions, and lifting inline literals into named globals.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: The dispatch structure looked like one or more big switch statements; reviewer asked to see whether a switch could match.
review_feedback.resolution: Author reported converting the reviewed ftCo_09F7 goto dispatch into a grouped switch while preserving matching behavior.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Do not use register, asm, or similar keywords.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Keep constants like strings and floats inlined; these are resolved during linking.
review_feedback.resolution: Author reported restoring inline float/string literals.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Same inline literal guidance; also remove the added optimizer pragma.
review_feedback.resolution: Author reported restoring inline literals and removing the pragma.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Revert string-global changes because they were less readable and would be resolved during linking.
review_feedback.resolution: Author reported restoring inline string literals.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Local verification after particle context-slot type fix: targeted particle.o build, full ninja, main.dol OK, and regression checks passed.
review_feedback.resolution: Verification evidence was posted before merge.

searchable_terms: Improve matching across multiple source files
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine THPFileInfo struct layout or remove THPDec view structs.
Search for remaining register keyword usage introduced for matching and whether reviewers allow any exceptions.
Search for grouped switch conversions in ftCo_09F7 or other fighter dispatch code.
Find PRs discussing inline string or float literal policy versus named rodata symbols.
Check whether particle context-slot type fixes from this PR were generalized into headers or baselib structs.
Search for remaining optimizer pragmas in source files and whether they are legacy-accepted or should be removed.
